auth_notes: Requires Oracle Hospitality Integration Platform (OHIP) credentials. Authentication involves a multi-step process: obtaining an OAuth 2.0 Bearer token using Client ID/Secret, which must then be sent alongside a mandatory 'x-app-key' header in all REST requests.
rate_limits: Rate limits are enforced by the Oracle Hospitality Integration Platform (OHIP) and vary based on the specific API tier and the hotel's subscription level.
gotchas: The API uses the OpenTravel Alliance (OTA) standard, which results in extremely verbose and deeply nested JSON structures.
Access requires an active OHIP subscription and developer portal approval, which can take time to provision.
Webhooks (Business Events) are managed via a Streaming API that may require separate configuration in the Oracle Cloud console.
research_notes: Integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. A dedicated workflow or 'Execute Workflow' node is recommended to handle the OAuth 2.0 token refresh logic, as the token must be paired with the static x-app-key. Data transformation will be heavy due to the OTA-compliant XML-to-JSON conversion used by Oracle.
